rationale: The citing paper uses Ahluwalia et al. (2001) to define what a spillover effect is and to justify that evaluations can transfer across mentally associated entities without a direct causal link. This definition is placed in the core conceptual development section that motivates the paper's main hypotheses about website-to-employee spillover. While the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's commitment mechanism, it relies on it as a key conceptual anchor for its spillover framing.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its central spillover argument on the conceptual definition from Ahluwalia et al. (2001), then extends that idea to digital service contexts (website cues spilling over to employee service quality perceptions).
